method_summary: SIRA is a training-free internal contrastive decoding framework that constructs a counterfactual reference inside the same LVLM by exploiting the staged information flow of multimodal transformers. It first lets image and text tokens interact through a shared prefix, forming an aligned multimodal state, then forks a counterfactual branch in later transformer layers where attention to image-token positions is masked.
key_innovation: The staged information flow approach that creates a counterfactual branch within the same LVLM without external tools or training, using a shared prefix for multimodal alignment followed by a masked branch for language-prior-dominated contrast.

key_results.overhead: lower overhead than two-pass contrastive decoding

best_result: Not numerically specified in the text
limitations_stated: The provided text doesn't explicitly state limitations of the approach.
title: Do We Really Need External Tools to Mitigate Hallucinations? SIRA: Shared-Prefix Internal Reconstruction of Attribution
